summary:
The gene most closely associated with odontoblast differentiation and dentin production is DEN, which encodes dentin sialophosphoprotein (DSP)—a key structural component of the dentin matrix secreted by odontoblasts. This makes DEN central to odontoblast function and dentinogenesis, even if no term precisely matches "odontoblast differentiation"; its role in mediating dentin secretion underpins its relevance.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$  
(No further explanation needed.)
